title: Guillermo Solovey
text:
Visiting Professor at the Business School of Torcuato Di Tella University, Independent Researcher at CONICET, and Professor at the University of Buenos Aires (currently on leave). In 2024 I served as Director of the Instituto de Cálculo.
My research is in cognitive science. A central theme is how people form and calibrate certainty — the internal signals that tell us how confident we are in what we see, remember, or choose. I study how awareness of our own mental states shapes behavior, and how the structure of a choice situation changes what we think we know: for instance, why adding irrelevant options can paradoxically increase confidence, or why the certainty we report after a decision draws on more information than what actually drove the choice. A second line of work asks how people evaluate contested claims: whether collective judgment can be used to fact-check political statements, and what computational processes underlie belief and disbelief in the face of potentially false information.
I hold a degree and PhD in Physics from the University of Buenos Aires. From 2009 to 2013 I was a postdoctoral fellow at Columbia University and The Rockefeller University in New York. My work has appeared in journals of experimental psychology, neuroscience, cognition, and consciousness.
As a teacher, I have taught undergraduate and graduate courses in statistics, data visualization, and behavioral science. I have also participated in training programs on statistical literacy for public officials and journalists.
Full CV

title: Guillermo Solovey
text:
I have taught undergraduate and graduate courses at the University of Buenos Aires and Torcuato Di Tella University, covering statistics, Bayesian inference, data visualization, and behavioral science. I have also led training workshops on statistical literacy for public officials and journalists. My teaching aims to connect rigorous quantitative methods with real-world reasoning and decision-making.

Interactive Apps
A collection of experiments and visualizations built for the course Neurociencia y Psicología Experimental at Torcuato Di Tella University. Try them in your browser — no installation needed.

Experiments

Two-Armed Bandit — Stroop — Gambling Game — Detection Threshold — Digit Memory



Visualizations & Games

Correlation — Random Assignment — Random Sampling — Set
